findings.claim: Worktree path fallback to active checkout is silent and unbounded.

fix_alternatives.label: A
fix_alternatives.description: Replace silent fallback with explicit error: return 1 with diagnostic.

fix_alternatives.label: B
fix_alternatives.description: Gate fallback behind a --allow-active-fallback opt-in flag.

findings.trigger: Any call to run-sandboxed.sh from a worker whose worktree was already cleaned.
findings.cascade_impact: Three callers in scripts/lib/ rely on the WT_CLEAN gate; silent fallback breaks all three.

findings.claim: Null guard at point of use covers one call site; two siblings call the same upstream factory.

evidence.quoted_line: return cls(maybe_none) if maybe_none else None

fix_alternatives.label: A
fix_alternatives.description: Move the null guard into the factory constructor so all 3 call sites benefit.

fix_alternatives.label: B
fix_alternatives.description: Replicate the guard at each call site (faster but doesn't fix root cause).

findings.cascade_impact: The two other callers in src/cli.py and src/api.py will still hit the null path because the guard is colocated with one consumer rather than the producer.

verdict.reasoning: One high-severity reliability bug (silent worktree fallback) and one root-cause-depth finding (null guard at the wrong layer). Both have verifiable evidence; the root-cause one carries cascade_impact as required. Address before merge.
